yacht: From Dutch 'jacht' (a hunt) — a fast ship; the spelling stayed Dutch.
rhythm: From Greek rhythmos — flow; famously written with no standard vowel.
silhouette: Named after Étienne de Silhouette, an 18th-century French minister.
schadenfreude: Borrowed whole from German: Schaden (harm) + Freude (joy).
onomatopoeia: Greek 'name-making' — words that sound like what they mean.
quinoa: From Quechua 'kinwa' — the Andean grain; spelling kept from the source.
mnemonic: From Mnemosyne, Greek goddess of memory — hence the silent m.
hierarchy: Greek hieros (sacred) + arkhia (rule) — a sacred order.
